description_long: This bird has a medium-sized, elongated body with a slightly hunched posture and a long, gently curved beak. Its plumage is predominantly brownish-gray, with subtle gradations and a somewhat matte finish, giving it a muted appearance. The head is rounded, with a distinct, slightly lighter area around the eye and a pronounced, downward-curving beak. The wings are folded tightly against the body, and the tail is long, narrow, and tapers to a subtle point. The legs are mostly hidden, but appear to be short and sturdy, likely gray or brown in color. The overall impression is of a bird adapted for foraging, possibly in wooded or urban park environments.
description: A medium-sized, brownish-gray bird with a long, curved beak and a tapered tail, featuring subtle feather gradations and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's elongated body, curved beak, and subtle feather gradations along the flank.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the narrow, tapered tail and the symmetry of the folded wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same elongated body and curved beak, with more visible feather layering on the side.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the rounded head, prominent beak, and the subtle feather pattern around the face.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid brownish-gray with subtle gradients and minimal patterning
